collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\n                 ORDER BY version DESC\n                 LIMIT 1\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        match rows.first() {\n            Some(row) => {\n                let schema_json: serde_json::Value = row.get(\"schema_json\");\n                let schema: CollectionSchema = serde_json::from_value(schema_json)?;\n                Ok(Some(schema))\n            }\n            None => Ok(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn get_schema_version(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        version: u32,\n    ) -> Result<Option<CollectionSchema>, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT schema_json FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3 AND version = $4\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &(version as i32),\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        match rows.first() {\n            Some(row) => {\n                let schema_json: serde_json::Value = row.get(\"schema_json\");\n                let schema: CollectionSchema = serde_json::from_value(schema_json)?;\n                Ok(Some(schema))\n            }\n            None => Ok(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn list_schema_versions(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<(u32, u64)>, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT version, created_at_ns FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\n                 ORDER BY version ASC\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        Ok(rows\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|row| {\n                (\n                    row.get::<_, i32>(\"version\") as u32,\n                    row.get::<_, i64>(\"created_at_ns\") as u64,\n                )\n            })\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    fn delete_schema(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn put_collection_view(&mut self, view: &CollectionView) -> Result<CollectionView, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(&view.collection)?;\n        if !self.collection_exists_in_namespace(&key.collection, &key.namespace)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n                \"collection '{}' is not registered\",\n                view.collection.as_str()\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let current_version = self\n            .block(self.client.query_opt(\n                \"SELECT version FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n                &[\n                    &key.collection.as_str(),\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?\n            .map_or(0, |row| row.get::<_, i32>(\"version\"));\n        let next_version = current_version + 1;\n\n        let updated_at_ns = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_nanos() as i64;\n\n        let mut versioned = view.clone();\n        versioned.collection = key.collection.clone();\n        versioned.version = next_version as u32;\n        versioned.updated_at_ns = Some(updated_at_ns as u64);\n        let view_json = serde_json::to_value(&versioned)?;\n\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO collection_views\n                    (collection, database_name, schema_name, version, view_json, updated_at_ns, updated_by)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7)\n                 ON CONFLICT (database_name, schema_name, collection) DO UPDATE SET\n                     version = EXCLUDED.version,\n                     view_json = EXCLUDED.view_json,\n                     updated_at_ns = EXCLUDED.updated_at_ns,\n                     updated_by = EXCLUDED.updated_by\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &next_version,\n                &view_json,\n                &updated_at_ns,\n                &versioned.updated_by,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(versioned)\n    }\n\n    fn get_collection_view(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> Result<Option<CollectionView>, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT view_json FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        match rows.first() {\n            Some(row) => {\n                let view_json: serde_json::Value = row.get(\"view_json\");\n                let view: CollectionView = serde_json::from_value(view_json)?;\n                Ok(Some(view))\n            }\n            None => Ok(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn delete_collection_view(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn register_collection_in_namespace(\n        &mut self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        if !self.namespace_exists(namespace)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\"namespace '{namespace}'\")));\n        }\n\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO collections (name, database_name, schema_name)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3)\n                 ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING\",\n            &[&collection.as_str(), &namespace.database, &namespace.schema],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn unregister_collection(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let raw_collection = collection.as_str();\n        let default_namespace = Namespace::default_ns();\n        // Upgraded databases may still have a pre-fix collection_views table\n        // without the collection -> collections foreign key, and may still\n        // carry metadata rows keyed by the original qualified identifier in\n        // either the resolved namespace or the old default/default namespace,\n        // so clean them up explicitly instead of relying solely on ON DELETE\n        // CASCADE.\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                     WHERE collection = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                     WHERE collection = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collections\n                 WHERE name = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM collections\n                     WHERE name = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn list_collections(&self) -> Result<Vec<CollectionId>, AxonError> {\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT name FROM collections\n                 ORDER BY database_name ASC, schema_name ASC, name ASC\",\n            &[],\n        ))?;\n\n        Ok(rows\n            .iter()\n            .map(|row| {\n                let name: String = row.get(\"name\");\n                CollectionId::new(name)\n            })\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    fn collection_registered_in_namespace(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<bool, AxonError> {\n        self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\n///\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    if name.is_empty() {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\n            \"database name must not be empty\".to_owned(),\n        ));\n    }\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\"non-empty checked above\");\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() && first != '_' {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\"\n        )));\n    }\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\"\n        )));\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\n/// `dbname` key.\n///\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: &str, tenant_db_name: &str) -> String {\n    let target = format!(\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\");\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgres://\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgresql://\") {\n        // URL format: replace path component.\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\"://\") {\n            let after_scheme = &superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\n                let scheme_authority = &superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\n                let rest = &after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\n                    &rest[q..]\n                } else {\n                    \"\"\n                };\n                return format!(\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\");\n            }\n            // No path: append the target database.\n            return format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\");\n        }\n        format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\")\n    } else {\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\n        let mut parts: Vec<String> = superadmin_dsn\n            .split_whitespace()\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\"dbname=\"))\n            .map(str::to_owned)\n            .collect();\n        parts.push(format!(\"dbname={target}\"));\n        parts.join(\" \")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\n/// superadmin connection.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\n///\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"CREATE DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if !exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"DROP DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\n// Explicit verification path:\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to provision PostgreSQL\n// via testcontainers and skip cleanly if the container runtime is unavailable.\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use std::{\n        ops::{Deref, DerefMut},\n        sync::{Mutex, MutexGuard, OnceLock},\n    };\n\n    use super::*;\n    use axon_core::types::Link;\n    use testcontainers_modules::postgres::Postgres as PgContainer;\n    use testcontainers_modules::testcontainers::{runners::SyncRunner, Container};\n\n    struct TestDatabase {\n        url: String,\n        _container: Option<Container<PgContainer>>,\n    }\n\n    enum TestSetupError {\n        Skip(String),\n        Fail(AxonError),\n    }\n\n    impl TestDatabase {\n        fn connect() -> Result<Self, TestSetupError> {\n            if let Ok(url) = std::env::var(\"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES\") {\n                return Ok(Self {\n                    url,\n                    _container: None,\n                });\n            }\n\n            let container = PgContainer::default()\n                .with_db_name(\"axon_test\")\n                .with_user(\"postgres\")\n                .with_password(\"postgres\")\n                .start()\n                .map_err(|error| {\n                    TestSetupError::Skip(format!(\n                        \"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES is unset and PostgreSQL test container startup failed: {error}\"\n                    ))\n                })?;\n            let host = container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n            let port = container.get_host_port_ipv4(5432).map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container port: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(Self {\n                url: format!(\n                    \"host={host} port={port} user=postgres password=postgres dbname=axon_test\"\n                ),\n                _container: Some(container),\n            })\n        }\n\n        fn url(&self) -> &str {\n            &self.url\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct TestStore {\n        adapter: PostgresStorageAdapter,\n        _database: TestDatabase,\n    }\n\n    impl Deref for TestStore {\n        type Target = PostgresStorageAdapter;\n\n        fn deref(&self) -> &Self::Target {\n            &self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\n        fn deref_mut(&mut self) -> &mut Self::Target {\n            &mut self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn postgres_test_guard() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()> {\n        static GUARD: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();\n        GUARD\n            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"PostgreSQL test guard lock should not be poisoned\")\n    }\n\n    fn skip_postgres_test(test_name: &str, reason: &str) {\n        tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\");\n    }\n\n    fn database_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestDatabase> {\n        match TestDatabase::connect() {\n            Ok(database) => Some(database),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn store() -> Result<TestStore, TestSetupError> {\n        let database = TestDatabase::connect()?;\n        let adapter =\n            PostgresStorageAdapter::connect(database.url()).map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        // Clean tables for a fresh test.\n        adapter\n            .block(adapter.client.batch_execute(\n                \"TRUNCATE entities, schemas, collection_views, collections, namespaces, databases, audit_log RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE\",\n            ))\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        adapter\n            .ensure_default_namespace()\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        Ok(TestStore {\n            adapter,\n            _database: database,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn store_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestStore> {\n        match store() {\n            Ok(store) => Some(store),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn register_unique_namespaced_collection(\n        store: &mut TestStore,\n        qualified: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> (Namespace, CollectionId) {\n        let (namespace, bare_collection) = Namespace::parse(qualified.as_str());\n        let bare_collection = CollectionId::new(bare_collection);\n\n        store\n            .create_database(namespace.database.as_str())\n            .expect(\"database create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .create_namespace(&namespace)\n            .expect(\"namespace create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .register_collection_in_namespace(&bare_collection, &namespace)\n            .expect(\"collection register should succeed\");\n\n tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"test runtime\");\n        let (client, connection) = rt\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(url, NoTls))\n            .expect(\"test connection\");\n        rt.spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        (client, rt)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn postgres_roundtrip_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"postgres_roundtrip_when_available\") else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let col = CollectionId::new(\"tasks\");\n        let entity = Entity::new(\n            col.clone(),\n            EntityId::new(\"t-001\"),\n            serde_json::json!({\"title\": \"hello\"}),\n        );\n        s.put(entity).expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        let got = s\n            .get(&col, &EntityId::new(\"t-001\"))\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\")\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        assert_eq!(got.data[\"title\"], \"hello\");\n        assert_eq!(got.version, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(database) = database_or_skip(\n            \"unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available\",\n        ) else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let (legacy_client, legacy_rt) = connect_raw_for_test(database.url());\n        legacy_rt\n            .block_on(async {\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collection_views;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collections;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS namespaces;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS databases;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS entities;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS schemas;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS audit_log;\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"CREATE TABLE collections (\n                            name TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY\n                        );\n                        CREATE TABLE collection_views (\n                            collection    TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,\n                            version       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                            view_json     JSONB NOT NULL,\n                            updated_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                            updated_by    TEXT\n                        );\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                Ok::<_, tokio_postgres::Error>(())\n            })\n            .expect(\"legacy schema should be created\");\n\n        let collection = CollectionId::new(\"ephemeral\");\n        let legacy_view = CollectionView {\n            collection: collection.clone(),\n            description: Some(\"legacy view\".into()),\n            markdown_template: \"# {{title}}\".into(),\n            version: 1,\n            updated_at_ns: Some(42),\n            updated_by: Some(\"legacy\".into()),\n        };\n        let legacy_view_json =\n            serde_json::to_value(&legacy_view).expect(\"legacy collection view should serialize\");\n\n        legacy_rt\n            .block_on(async {\n                legacy_client\n                    .execute(\n                        \"INSERT INTO collections (name) VALUES ($1)\",\n                        &[&collection.as_str()],\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                legacy_client\n                    .execute(\n                        \"INSERT INTO collection_views (collection, version, view_json, updated_at_ns, Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\n//! 9. TenantId, UserId};\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\npub struct JwtIssuer {\n    secret: Vec<u8>,\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\n    pub issuer_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl JwtIssuer {\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec<u8>, issuer_id: String) -> Self {\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\n    }\n\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\n    pub fn issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError> {\n        encode(\n            &Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\n            claims,\n            &EncodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret),\n        )\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n    }\n\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\n    ///\n    /// Result<JwtClaims, AuthError> {\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\n\n        decode::<JwtClaims>(token, &DecodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret), &validation)\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature => AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                _ => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n            })\n    }\n}\n\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\n///\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\n#[derive(Default)]\npub struct Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\n    pub fn contains(&self, jti: &Uuid) -> bool {\n        self.inner\n            .read()\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\n    pub fn insert(&self, jti: Uuid) {\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\n            g.insert(jti);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\n///\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\n///\n/// Install with:\n/// ```ignore\n/// ```\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\n    State(state): State<Arc<AuthPipelineState>>,\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(&path) else {\n        return next.run(request).await;\n    };\n\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\n    let database = database.to_string();\n    let method = request.method().clone();\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\n\n    match verify_request(&state, &headers, &method, &tenant, &database) {\n        Ok(identity) => {\n            let mut request = request;\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\n            next.run(request).await\n        }\n        Err(err) => AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfn verify_request(\n    state: &AuthPipelineState,\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n    method: &Method,\n    tenant: &str,\n    database: &str,\n) -> Result<ResolvedIdentity, AuthError> {\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\n\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\n    let claims = with 30 s skew.\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs();\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\n    // Expired: exp + skew < now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) < now {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\n    }\n    // Not yet valid: nbf > now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\n    if claims.nbf > now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\n    }\n\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\n        .jti\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(&jti_uuid) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n    }\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        if storage\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\n        {\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\n            drop(storage);\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\n    }\n\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\n    let user_id = UserId::new(&claims.sub);\n    let user = {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n    };\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\n    }\n\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\n    }\n\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\n    let granted_db = claims\n        .grants\n        .find_database(database)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\n\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\n        Op::Read\n    } else {\n        Op::Write\n    };\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\n    }\n\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\n        user_id,\n        tenant_id,\n        grants: claims.grants,\n &HeaderMap) -> Result<&str, AuthError> {\n    let auth_header = headers\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n\n    let auth_str = auth_header\n (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\n        (Some(\"tenants\"), Some(tenant), Some(\"databases\"), Some(rest))\n            if !tenant.is_empty() && !rest.is_empty() =>\n        {\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\n            if database.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some((tenant, database))\n            }\n into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n        let body = json!({\n            \"error\": {\n                \"code\": self.0.error_code(),\n                \"message\": self.0.to_string()\n            }\n        });\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\n    .to_str()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n\n    auth_str\n        .strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n}\n\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\n///\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)> {\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\n        (Some(\"tenants\"), Some(tenant), Some(\"databases\"), Some(rest))\n            if !tenant.is_empty() && !rest.is_empty() =>\n        {\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\n            if database.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some((tenant, database))\n            }\n        }\n        _ => None,\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\n\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\n    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n        let body = json!({\n            \"error\": {\n                \"code\": self.0.error_code(),\n                \"message\": self.0.to_string()\n            }\n        });\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n//!\n//! # Authentication\n//!\n//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n//!\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n//!\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n//!\n//! # Request flow\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! TCP accept\n//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n//!             └─ route handler\n//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n//!                  └─ handler logic\n//! ```\n//!\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\npub mod actor_scope;\npub mod auth;\nmod collection_listing;\npub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\npub mod control_plane;\npub mod control_plane_routes;\npub mod cors_config;\npub mod user_roles;\npub mod gateway;\npub mod idempotency;\npub(crate) mod mcp_http;\npub mod mcp_stdio;\npub mod rate_limit;\npub mod schema_registry;\npub mod serve;\npub mod service;\npub mod tenant_router;\n\npub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\npub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\npub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":43,"oldLines":6,"newStart":43,"newLines":7,"lines":[" //! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`]."," pub mod actor_scope;"," pub mod auth;","+pub mod auth_pipeline;"," mod collection_listing;"," pub(crate) mod embedded_ui;"," pub mod control_plane;"]},{"oldStart":60,"oldLines":5,"newStart":61,"newLines":6,"lines":[" pub mod tenant_router;"," "," pub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};","+pub use auth_pipeline::{InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};"," pub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;"," pub use service::AxonServiceImpl;"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01CK6wwgEq2n1LP4QVdfHsPL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01G11hf2mEssXRvin3Et23Gc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs","content":"//! Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BcRLMXUs4TZUS5Hhv4m4p8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01QqPQtyiL6uPht7wAo243yE","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs","content":"//! Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":575,"cache_read_input_tokens":132304,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":575},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"cc1c03fa-01b8-4915-97d1-edc4d799b3f6"}

Embedded admin UI — assets from `ui/build` compiled into the binary.\n//!\n//! The `UiAssets` struct embeds every file under `ui/build` at compile time.\n//! The axum handler serves them at `/ui/*path` with correct `Content-Type`\n//! headers, and falls back to `index.html` for any unrecognised path so that\n//! SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n//!\n//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n\n/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n///\n/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\nstruct UiAssets;\n\n/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n///\n/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

     (`TestPromptSizeReport`) renders both variants
     (claude harness, agent harness) at contextBudget = "" (full) and
     "minimal", logs the table to test output, writes a tab-separated
     human report and a JSON sibling, and asserts minimal < full bytes
     per variant to catch cheap-tier regressions.
   - Determinism guard: `TestPromptSHA_DeterministicAcrossRenders`
     asserts the helper is stable across renders and differs across
     variants.

3. CI surfaces the report as an artifact
   - .github/workflows/ci.yml:201-214 runs the fixture with
     `DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT=${{ github.workspace }}/prompt-size-report
     .txt` and uploads `prompt-size-report.{txt,json,log}` as the
     `prompt-size-report` artifact.

Verification run in this worktree
---------------------------------
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run
'TestPromptSizeReport|TestPromptSHA|ExecuteBeadArtifacts' -count=1` →
ok (0.012s).

Why the bead was re-queued
--------------------------
The notes section on this bead reads "REVIEW:BLOCK — Diff contains only
this attempt's own execution evidence ... The shown manifest.json itself
lacks a prompt_sha field, contradicting AC7." That review fired against
a *different* attempt whose diff happened to contain only the evidence
files (manifest.json/result.json) for that attempt's own execution
bundle. The actual implementation landed on a separate commit
(efbed403) which is now on main. The recorded manifest.json the
reviewer inspected was generated by an *earlier* execution that ran
before efbed403 was merged, so it predates the field — the manifest.json
emitted by the current binary does include prompt_sha.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
Nothing for AC7. If the reviewer still doubts the field is being
written, point them at the fresh manifest.json in this attempt's
evidence directory (.ddx/executions/20260502T121152-24b051ff/
manifest.json) and the unit test
TestExecuteBeadArtifacts_ManifestPromptSHA in execute_bead_artifacts_
test.go which asserts prompt_sha is present and matches sha256 of
prompt.md.",

compute the quantiles and surface existing single-class counts, then
be extended when G lands.

  aggregates the review-outcomes data from bead events and attempt
  bundles): compute prompt_size_p50, prompt_size_p95, prompt_size_p99
  over input_bytes values collected from review events in the window;
  compute failure_classes counts keyed on context_overflow,
  provider_empty, unparseable, transport.

Out-of-scope:
- A new CLI command (FEAT-022 §17 explicitly says "no new command").
- Any web UI consumer of the new fields.
- GraphQL exposure of the aggregated metrics — separate work if
  needed.

remain unchanged.",

data.text: I have enough context to edit now. I’m adding three connected pieces in one pass: persist `prompt_sha` into session index records, expose a `promptSha` GraphQL filter on the efficacy resolvers, and make the prompt-size CI test fail on a byte budget instead of only reporting.

The source changes are in place. I’m regenerating the GraphQL bindings next so the schema, resolver signatures, and generated interfaces stay consistent instead of hand-editing the large generated file.

data.text: The schema/codegen change is mechanical but it did surface one real follow-up: every direct `queryResolver.EfficacyRows/EfficacyAttempts` call now needs the extra `promptSha` argument. I’m patching those call sites now, then I’ll rerun formatting and tests.
I’ve finished the code changes and formatting. Next I’m running the two affected package test sets to catch any remaining signature or schema mismatches before I look at anything else.

data.text: The repo hook itself is failing on `go test -race` because cgo is off in this environment, not because of the code changes. I’m checking whether a cgo-enabled rerun is enough to clear the hook; if not, I’ll report the exact gate failure instead of pretending it passed.

data.output:
# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
    go-fmt:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Format"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
          exit 1
        fi
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
    go-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Lint"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
        for pkg in $packages; do
          golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go build - only for changed packages
    go-build:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Build"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: 'go build -v ./...'
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
    evidence-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."

    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
    runtime-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."

    # Go tests - targeted testing
    go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      env:
        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"

    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
    secrets:
      <<: *security_checks
      name: "Scan for secrets"
      run: |
        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
        else
          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')

  1279		return artifacts, nil

   157	func SessionIndexEntryFromResult(projectRoot string, inputs SessionIndexInputs, result *Result, startedAt, endedAt time.Time) SessionIndexEntry {
   158		if result == nil {
   159			result = &Result{}
   160		}
   161		corr := inputs.Correlation
   162		id := ""
   163		beadID := ""
   164		workerID := ""
   165		bundlePath := ""
   166		baseRev := ""
   167		nativeLogRef := ""
   168		nativeSessionID := ""
   169		traceID := ""
   170		spanID := ""
   171		if corr != nil {
   172			id = corr["session_id"]
   173			beadID = corr["bead_id"]
   174			workerID = corr["worker_id"]
   175			bundlePath = corr["bundle_path"]
   176			baseRev = corr["base_rev"]
   177			nativeLogRef = corr["native_log_ref"]
   178			nativeSessionID = corr["native_session_id"]
   179			traceID = corr["trace_id"]
   180			spanID = corr["span_id"]
   181		}
   182		if id == "" {
   183			id = genSessionID()
   184		}
   185		if bundlePath == "" && corr != nil && corr["attempt_id"] != "" {
   186			bundlePath = filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, corr["attempt_id"])
   187		}
   188		if nativeLogRef == "" && result.AgentSessionID != "" {
   189			nativeLogRef = relativizePath(projectRoot, result.AgentSessionID)
   190		}
   191		if nativeSessionID == "" {
   192			nativeSessionID = result.AgentSessionID
   193		}
   194		harness := result.Harness
   195		if harness == "" {
   196			harness = inputs.Harness
   197		}
   198		model := result.Model
   199		if model == "" {
   200			model = inputs.Model
   201		}
   202		outcome := "success"
   203		if result.ExitCode != 0 || result.Error != "" {
   204			outcome = "failure"
   205		}
   206		return SessionIndexEntry{
   207			ID:              id,
   208			ProjectID:       ProjectIDForPath(projectRoot),
   209			BeadID:          beadID,
   210			WorkerID:        workerID,
   211			Harness:         harness,
   212			Provider:        firstNonEmpty(result.Provider, inputs.Provider),
   213			BaseURL:         result.ResolvedBaseURL,
   214			BillingMode:     billingModeFor(harness, "", result.ResolvedBaseURL),
   215			Model:           model,
   216			PromptSHA:       corr["prompt_sha"],
   217			StartedAt:       startedAt.UTC(),
   218			EndedAt:         endedAt.UTC(),
   219			DurationMS:      int(endedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
   220			CostUSD:         result.CostUSD,
   221			CostPresent:     result.CostUSD != 0,
   222			Tokens:          result.Tokens,
   223			InputTokens:     result.InputTokens,
   224			CachedTokens:    result.CachedTokens,
   225			OutputTokens:    result.OutputTokens,
   226			Outcome:         outcome,
   227			ExitCode:        result.ExitCode,
   228			NativeSessionID: nativeSessionID,
   229			TraceID:         traceID,
   230			SpanID:          spanID,
   510			}
   511		}
   512		if bodies.Stderr == "" && entry.Detail != "" && entry.Outcome == "failure" {
   513			bodies.Stderr = entry.Detail
   514		}
   515		return bodies
   516	}

   518	func SessionIndexEntryToLegacy(e SessionIndexEntry) SessionEntry {

Implemented and committed as `d18c419d` with subject `feat(agent): add prompt_sha manifest and efficacy slicing [ddx-eddb9ab6]`.

- `prompt_sha` now flows from the rendered prompt into the manifest and session index, and is preserved through legacy conversion in [execute_bead.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eddb9ab6-20260505T204143-5944699b/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:192) and [session_index.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eddb9ab6-20260505T204143-5944699b/cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:35).
- `efficacyRows` and `efficacyAttempts` now accept an optional `promptSha` filter in [schema.graphql](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eddb9ab6-20260505T204143-5944699b/cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2898), [resolver_feat008.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eddb9ab6-20260505T204143-5944699b/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:349), and the resolver test coverage in [efficacy_sessions_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eddb9ab6-20260505T204143-5944699b/cli/internal/server/graphql/efficacy_sessions_test.go:304).
- The prompt-size CI fixture now enforces byte ceilings instead of only reporting, in [execute_bead_promptsize_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eddb9ab6-20260505T204143-5944699b/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_promptsize_test.go:66).
- Added a session-index roundtrip test for `prompt_sha` in [session_index_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eddb9ab6-20260505T204143-5944699b/cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go:214).

Verification:
- `go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1`
- `go test ./internal/server/graphql/... -count=1`
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`
